speaker: PUCELLE
text: The Regent conquers and the Frenchmen fly.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Now help, you charming spells and periapts,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And you choice spirits that admonish me,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And give me signs of future accidents.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: You speedy helpers, that are substitutes

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Under the lordly monarch of the north,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Appear, and aid me in this enterprise.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: This speed and quick appearance argues proof

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Of your accustomed diligence to me.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Now, you familiar spirits that are culled

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Out of the powerful regions under earth,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Help me this once, that France may get the field.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: O, hold me not with silence overlong!

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Where I was wont to feed you with my blood,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: I’ll lop a member off and give it you

speaker: PUCELLE
text: In earnest of a further benefit,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: So you do condescend to help me now.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: No hope to have redress? My body shall

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Pay recompense if you will grant my suit.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Cannot my body nor blood-sacrifice

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Entreat you to your wonted furtherance?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Then take my soul—my body, soul, and all—

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Before that England give the French the foil.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: See, they forsake me. Now the time is come

speaker: PUCELLE
text: That France must vail her lofty-plumèd crest

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And let her head fall into England’s lap.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: My ancient incantations are too weak,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And hell too strong for me to buckle with.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Now, France, thy glory droopeth to the dust.

speaker: YORK
text: Damsel of France, I think I have you fast.

speaker: YORK
text: Unchain your spirits now with spelling charms,

speaker: YORK
text: And try if they can gain your liberty.

speaker: YORK
text: A goodly prize, fit for the devil’s grace!

speaker: YORK
text: See how the ugly witch doth bend her brows

speaker: YORK
text: As if with Circe she would change my shape.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Changed to a worser shape thou canst not be.

speaker: YORK
text: O, Charles the Dauphin is a proper man;

speaker: YORK
text: No shape but his can please your dainty eye.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: A plaguing mischief light on Charles and thee,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And may you both be suddenly surprised

speaker: PUCELLE
text: By bloody hands in sleeping on your beds!

speaker: YORK
text: Fell banning hag! Enchantress, hold thy tongue.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: I prithee give me leave to curse awhile.

speaker: YORK
text: Curse, miscreant, when thou com’st to the stake.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Be what thou wilt, thou art my prisoner.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: O fairest beauty, do not fear nor fly,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: For I will touch thee but with reverent hands.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I kiss these fingers for eternal peace

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And lay them gently on thy tender side.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Who art thou? Say, that I may honor thee.

speaker: MARGARET
text: Margaret my name, and daughter to a king,

speaker: MARGARET
text: The King of Naples, whosoe’er thou art.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: An earl I am, and Suffolk am I called.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Be not offended, nature’s miracle;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thou art allotted to be ta’en by me.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: So doth the swan her downy cygnets save,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Keeping them prisoner underneath her wings.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Yet if this servile usage once offend,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Go and be free again as Suffolk’s friend.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text:
O, stay!
                 
                (
                Aside
                .
                )
               I have no power to let her pass.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: My hand would free her, but my heart says no.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As plays the sun upon the glassy streams,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Twinkling another counterfeited beam,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: So seems this gorgeous beauty to mine eyes.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Fain would I woo her, yet I dare not speak.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I’ll call for pen and ink and write my mind.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Fie, de la Pole, disable not thyself!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Hast not a tongue? Is she not here?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Wilt thou be daunted at a woman’s sight?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Ay. Beauty’s princely majesty is such

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Confounds the tongue and makes the senses rough.

speaker: MARGARET
text: Say, Earl of Suffolk, if thy name be so,

speaker: MARGARET
text: What ransom must I pay before I pass?

speaker: MARGARET
text: For I perceive I am thy prisoner.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: How canst thou tell she will deny thy suit

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Before thou make a trial of her love?

speaker: MARGARET
text: Why speak’st thou not? What ransom must I pay?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: She’s beautiful, and therefore to be wooed;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: She is a woman, therefore to be won.

speaker: MARGARET
text: Wilt thou accept of ransom, yea or no?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Fond man, remember that thou hast a wife;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Then how can Margaret be thy paramour?

speaker: MARGARET
text: I were best to leave him, for he will not hear.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: There all is marred; there lies a cooling card.

speaker: MARGARET
text: He talks at random; sure the man is mad.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And yet a dispensation may be had.

speaker: MARGARET
text: And yet I would that you would answer me.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I’ll win this Lady Margaret. For whom?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Why, for my king. Tush, that’s a wooden thing!

speaker: MARGARET
text: He talks of wood. It is some carpenter.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Yet so my fancy may be satisfied,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And peace establishèd between these realms.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: But there remains a scruple in that, too;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: For though her father be the King of Naples,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Duke of Anjou and Maine, yet is he poor,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And our nobility will scorn the match.

speaker: MARGARET
text: Hear you, captain? Are you not at leisure?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: It shall be so, disdain they ne’er so much.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Henry is youthful, and will quickly yield.—

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Madam, I have a secret to reveal.

speaker: MARGARET
text: What though I be enthralled, he seems a knight,

speaker: MARGARET
text: And will not any way dishonor me.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Lady, vouchsafe to listen what I say.

speaker: MARGARET
text: Perhaps I shall be rescued by the French,

speaker: MARGARET
text: And then I need not crave his courtesy.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Sweet madam, give me hearing in a cause.

speaker: MARGARET
text: Tush, women have been captivate ere now.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Lady, wherefore talk you so?

speaker: MARGARET
text: I cry you mercy, ’tis but quid for quo.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Say, gentle princess, would you not suppose

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Your bondage happy, to be made a queen?

speaker: MARGARET
text: To be a queen in bondage is more vile

speaker: MARGARET
text: Than is a slave in base servility,

speaker: MARGARET
text: For princes should be free.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And so shall you,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: If happy England’s royal king be free.

speaker: MARGARET
text: Why, what concerns his freedom unto me?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I’ll undertake to make thee Henry’s queen,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To put a golden scepter in thy hand

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And set a precious crown upon thy head,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: If thou wilt condescend to be my—

speaker: MARGARET
text: What?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: His love.

speaker: MARGARET
text: I am unworthy to be Henry’s wife.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: No, gentle madam, I unworthy am

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To woo so fair a dame to be his wife,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And have no portion in the choice myself.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: How say you, madam? Are you so content?

speaker: MARGARET
text: An if my father please, I am content.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Then call our captains and our colors forth!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And, madam, at your father’s castle walls

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: We’ll crave a parley to confer with him.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: See, Reignier, see thy daughter prisoner!

speaker: REIGNIER
text: To whom?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To me.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Suffolk, what remedy?

speaker: REIGNIER
text: I am a soldier and unapt to weep

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Or to exclaim on Fortune’s fickleness.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Yes, there is remedy enough, my lord:

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Consent, and, for thy Honor give consent,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thy daughter shall be wedded to my king,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Whom I with pain have wooed and won thereto;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And this her easy-held imprisonment

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Hath gained thy daughter princely liberty.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Speaks Suffolk as he thinks?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Fair Margaret knows

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That Suffolk doth not flatter, face, or feign.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Upon thy princely warrant, I descend

speaker: REIGNIER
text: To give thee answer of thy just demand.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And here I will expect thy coming.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Welcome, brave earl, into our territories.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Command in Anjou what your Honor pleases.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thanks, Reignier, happy for so sweet a child,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Fit to be made companion with a king.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: What answer makes your Grace unto my suit?

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Since thou dost deign to woo her little worth

speaker: REIGNIER
text: To be the princely bride of such a lord,

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Upon condition I may quietly

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Enjoy mine own, the country Maine and Anjou,

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Free from oppression or the stroke of war,

speaker: REIGNIER
text: My daughter shall be Henry’s, if he please.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That is her ransom; I deliver her,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And those two counties I will undertake

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Your Grace shall well and quietly enjoy.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: And I, again in Henry’s royal name

speaker: REIGNIER
text: As deputy unto that gracious king,

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Give thee her hand for sign of plighted faith.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Reignier of France, I give thee kingly thanks

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Because this is in traffic of a king.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              And yet methinks I could be well content

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To be mine own attorney in this case.—

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I’ll over then to England with this news,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And make this marriage to be solemnized.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: So farewell, Reignier; set this diamond safe

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: In golden palaces, as it becomes.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: I do embrace thee, as I would embrace

speaker: REIGNIER
text: The Christian prince King Henry, were he here.

speaker: MARGARET
text: Farewell, my lord; good wishes, praise, and prayers

speaker: MARGARET
text: Shall Suffolk ever have of Margaret.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Farewell, sweet madam. But, hark you, Margaret,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: No princely commendations to my king?

speaker: MARGARET
text: Such commendations as becomes a maid,

speaker: MARGARET
text: A virgin, and his servant, say to him.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Words sweetly placed and modestly directed.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: But, madam, I must trouble you again:

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: No loving token to his Majesty?

speaker: MARGARET
text: Yes, my good lord: a pure unspotted heart,

speaker: MARGARET
text: Never yet taint with love, I send the King.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And this withal.

speaker: MARGARET
text: That for thyself. I will not so presume

speaker: MARGARET
text: To send such peevish tokens to a king.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: O, wert thou for myself! But, Suffolk, stay.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thou mayst not wander in that labyrinth.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: There Minotaurs and ugly treasons lurk.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Solicit Henry with her wondrous praise;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Bethink thee on her virtues that surmount

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And natural graces that extinguish art;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Repeat their semblance often on the seas,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That, when thou com’st to kneel at Henry’s feet,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thou mayst bereave him of his wits with wonder.
